tasks.title: Enhance Codex runner streaming output with JSON events
evidence: User request: "Enahnce the streaming output for the codex runner. Switch to using 'codex exec --json' and capture the reasoning and agent_message. Maintain the streaming output. Run 'codex exec --help' to see the menu of options. User has captured 'codex exec --json "just say hi"' in a file located at /tmp/codex-exec.log"
plan: Locate Codex runner streaming implementation and identify where stdout/stderr events are parsed
Run `codex exec --help` and `codex exec --json "just say hi"` to capture supported flags and JSONL event shape
Switch runner to `codex exec --json` and stream JSONL events while preserving existing streaming UX
Extract and surface `reasoning` and `agent_message` fields in output/logs with clear formatting
Add/extend tests to cover JSONL parsing, streaming behavior, and failure modes
tasks.request: Enahnce the streaming output for the codex runner. Switch to using 'codex exec --json' and capture the reasoning and agent_message. Maintain the streaming output. Run 'codex exec --help' to see the menu of options. Run 'codex exec --json "just say hi"' to see a sample output.
